articles.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposes Private Container Images without Authentication
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security flaw in Gitea, an open-source, self-hosted platform for version control, that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to pull private container images from Gitea deployments without requiring an account, password, or other credentials. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-27771 (CVSS score: N/A), affects all versions of Gitea prior to 1.26.2

articles.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
articles.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

articles.summary: CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent software chain campaign targeting software developers through malicious packages and extensions. articles.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
articles.summary: The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026. The activity targeted industrial and electronics manufacturing, education and public-sector bodies, financial services, and professional services, per the Threat Hunter Team from Symantec and Carbon Black.

articles.summary: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued new guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical security vulnerabilities in internet-exposed systems within 12 hours of being flagged where "feasible" to safeguard against potential threats stemming from threat actors' abuse of artificial intelligence (AI) tools and large language models (LLMs) to automate vulnerability

articles.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
articles.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

articles.title: Why temperature records are being not only broken but smashed
articles.summary: The combined effects of a heat dome and climate change have brought extreme warmth to western Europe.

articles.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
articles.summary: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.

articles.title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, roughly 12,000 light-years in diameter and some 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. articles.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
articles.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

articles.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
articles.summary: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.

articles.title: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat

articles.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
articles.summary: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.

articles.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of world order, warns UN chief
articles.summary: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.

articles.title: AI Chatbot Recommendations Redirect Users to Cryptojacking Malware Sites
articles.summary: Microsoft has warned of an active cryptojacking campaign that makes use of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot interactions as a mechanism for surfacing malicious download sites. articles.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
articles.summary: Microsoft has rolled out updates to fix a remote code execution vulnerability impacting SharePoint that could be exploited by bad actors in attacks without requiring any specialized conditions to be met. articles.summary: Israel says it struck 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and fighters in Lebanon, after PM Benjamin Netanyahu vows to "crush" the Iran-backed group.

articles.title: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza
articles.summary: Intensified Israeli airstrikes overnight in Lebanon forced people to again flee their homes, while humanitarians in the Gaza Strip report continued restrictions in bringing aid into the enclave, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
articles.summary: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confidence in the Security Council’s ability to respond to international crises.

articles.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
articles.summary: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.

articles.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
articles.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

articles.title: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
articles.summary: Nasa plans to send hopping drones and roving vehicles to the Moon as part of plans for a permanent Moon base.

articles.title: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
articles.summary: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus, said on Monday.

articles.title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
articles.summary: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.
